skill.name: scientific-critical-thinking
skill.description: Evaluate scientific claims and evidence quality using structured checks for study design, bias, statistics, fallacies, and evidence strength.
skill.summary: Assess scientific claims, study design, bias, statistics, and evidence quality with structured critical thinking frameworks.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: The static weak-cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from scientific terminology in Markdown reference material. The only confirmed risk factor is external command guidance: the skill allows Bash and includes local command examples, with no evidence of malicious intent or data exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: External Command Capability Requires Caution
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash support and includes shell command examples for schematic generation and reference searching. These commands are documented local workflows, not hidden execution, but users should review commands before running them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and command examples are explicit, so the capability is confirmed. Risk is moderate because the examples are static documentation and show no credential access, obfuscation, or network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-volume weak cryptography alerts occur in scientific Markdown content about evidence, bias, study design, and statistical confidence. No evidence found of cryptographic APIs, weak hashes, encryption routines, or security-sensitive algorithm choices.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are explanatory research references, and inspected lines contain scientific methodology language. No code or cryptographic operation is present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Research Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance alerts map to benign research terms such as case-control designs, literature gaps, validity, and authority. No evidence found of host probing, network scanning, service discovery, or system inventory behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines are part of scientific review checklists and fallacy definitions. They do not contain executable discovery commands, network targets, or system enumeration logic.

low_findings.title: Optional External Platform Recommendation
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not malicious, but it is an external service recommendation that marketplace reviewers may want disclosed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external recommendation is explicit and limited to complex workflows. Confidence is moderate because there is no instruction to transmit user data or make automatic network requests.

content.value_statement: Scientific claims often mix strong evidence with flawed methods. This skill guides structured review of bias, design, statistics, and evidence strength.
actual_capabilities: Reviews research methods for validity threats and design weaknesses.
Identifies common biases, confounders, and flawed causal reasoning.
Checks statistical interpretation, including power, p-values, effect sizes, and confidence intervals.
Applies evidence hierarchy, GRADE, and risk-of-bias concepts to scientific claims.
Explains logical fallacies in scientific arguments with constructive corrections.
Suggests study design improvements for clearer hypotheses, controls, measurements, and analysis plans.
limitations: It does not replace expert peer review or domain-specific regulatory review.
It cannot verify unpublished data, hidden protocols, or unavailable analysis code.
It depends on the quality and completeness of the study materials provided.
It provides methodological critique, not medical, legal, or investment advice.

use_cases.title: Screen a Research Paper
use_cases.description: Assess whether methods, statistics, and conclusions support the main claim before relying on a paper.

use_cases.title: Improve a Study Protocol
use_cases.description: Find design gaps, bias risks, weak measures, and unclear analysis plans before data collection starts.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Public Science Claims
use_cases.description: Separate strong evidence from overstatement in press releases, articles, and expert commentary.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Claim Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one scientific claim.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this scientific claim. Identify the evidence offered, the type of claim, major assumptions, and what would strengthen or weaken it.

prompt_templates.title: Paper Methods Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured critique of a study or manuscript.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this paper for study design, sampling, controls, measurement quality, bias, confounding, statistical interpretation, and conclusion strength.

prompt_templates.title: Evidence Quality Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these studies using evidence hierarchy and GRADE-style reasoning. Explain which conclusions are best supported and where uncertainty remains.

prompt_templates.title: Protocol Stress Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Stress test this study protocol. Identify validity threats, bias controls, power and analysis issues, preregistration needs, and concrete design improvements.

output_examples.input: A paper claims a supplement improves cognition based on a small uncontrolled before-after study.
output_examples.output: The output would flag weak causal support, regression to the mean, placebo effects, no control group, small sample risk, and overstrong conclusions.

output_examples.input: A randomized trial reports a significant p-value but no effect size or confidence interval.
output_examples.output: The output would explain why statistical significance is incomplete and request effect sizes, precision estimates, assumptions, and practical importance.

output_examples.input: A team shares a draft protocol for an observational cohort study.
output_examples.output: The output would review exposure definitions, confounder plans, missing data handling, preregistration, measurement validity, and limits on causal language.

best_practices: Provide the claim, study type, methods, results, and stated limitations together.
Ask for severity-ranked concerns and actionable fixes, not only a flaw list.
Use the skill before finalizing protocols, conclusions, or public summaries.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to prove a preferred conclusion while ignoring contrary evidence.
Do not treat a short critique as a substitute for domain expert review.
Do not provide only abstracts when detailed methods or statistics are needed.

faq.question: Can this skill peer review a manuscript?
faq.answer: It can support methodological critique, but formal peer review needs domain experts and journal-specific standards.

faq.question: Does it use GRADE and Cochrane concepts?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for evidence hierarchy, GRADE-style quality judgments, and risk-of-bias reasoning.

faq.question: Can it design a new experiment?
faq.answer: It can suggest design improvements, controls, measurements, and analysis planning. It does not guarantee feasibility or ethics approval.

faq.question: Can it analyze raw data?
faq.answer: It focuses on critical review and interpretation. Raw statistical analysis depends on the tools and data you provide.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with confidential research?
faq.answer: Follow your organization rules for sensitive data. Remove private participant details before sharing materials with any AI tool.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Full methods, results, tables, protocol details, analysis plans, and the exact claim being evaluated work best.
